LoweringException: AssertionError: Expect scale_a and scale_b to be either both scalars (including single-element tensors) or 1-d

sglang.title: Fix loading KV quantization scale; Enable modelopt kv cache
sglang.author: yundai424
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Currently kv cache quantization is not usable because 1) we never pass layer.k/v_scale to attention backend, 2) we never load the precomputed kv scale.

Modifications

Add logics to load precomputed kv scale if present in modelopt
pass RadixAttention's k/v scale to flashinfer & FA3 backen

sglang.title: Add optimized native kernels in sgl-kernel
sglang.author: mingfeima
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This pull request is a follow up on #2807 to enable and optimize sglang performance on CPU devices. In this patch, optimized C++ kernels are provided including:

activations
layernorms
gemm (bfloat16, int8)
extend attention (bfloat16)
decode attention (bfloat16)
allreduce and allgather
m

sglang.title: [Bugfix] Fix index out of bounds in local attention with large sequences
sglang.author: CatherineSue
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Fix #5170

Modifications
Add critical safety check using np.clip to ensure block indices never exceed block_table dimensions. This fixes index errors that occurred with sequences slightly larger than the attention chunk size (e.g., 8193 tokens with 8192 chunk size) where the second local

vllm.title: [Misc] Avoid stripping meaningful whitespace from `nvidia-smi topo -m` output in collect_env.py
vllm.author: imkero
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The collect_env.py script runs nvidia-smi topo -m to gather GPU topology information. However, when processing the output, it applies .strip(), removing leading whitespace.
This causes a formatting issue because the output of nvidia-smi topo -m uses leading whitespace for table header's alignment.
C

vllm.author: jinzhen-lin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: #13232 scales the hidden states and residual of deepseek model to avoid the bf16 overflow issue of deepseek-v2. However, it only consider the case when first_k_dense_replace == 1. With models that use first_k_dense_replace > 1 (DeepSeek-R1 use 3), residual *= 1. / self.routed_scaling_factor would ru

vllm.title: [V1] Update structured output offline inference example
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Update this example to work with V1. The grammar specified was not
comaptible with xgrammar. This worked with V0 as we would automatically
fall back to outlines.
In V1 we have fallback support, but you have to opt-in to it by setting
your backend to auto. Grammar type support varies among the backen

This PR fixes two bugs:

TypeError: ROCmFlashAttentionImpl.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'use_irope'

vllm.title: [Core] Upgrade to xgrammar 0.1.18, add cache size limit
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
xgrammar 0.1.18 includes a fix for security vulnerability where a
malicious user can cause vllm to consume all of the host's RAM with
the knowledge that each unique grammar adds an entry to an unbounded
cache in memory.
The cache was implemented in xgrammar PR 243:
mlc-ai/xgrammar#243
This commit ma

vllm.title: [CI][Bugfix] Fix bad tolerance for test_batch_base64_embedding
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previously the test was doing equality on float values, which is always a bad idea. See an example of flaky test https://buildkite.com/vllm/fastcheck/builds/19776/steps?jid=0196126c-0cdf-4b29-9363-d925adaf681a#0196126c-0cdf-4b29-9363-d925adaf681a/6-13621
This PR changes it to use allclose
